verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a type I integral membrane protein with documented constitutive cycling between the trans-Golgi network (TGN) and the plasma membrane/cell surface via early endosomes. Although its steady-state localization is predominantly at the TGN, the protein routinely transits to and resides on the outer leaflet via its own transmembrane anchor. This trafficking cycling qualifies as a form of dual localization—the protein has a documented, stable PM pool as part of its normal biological function, not transient or conditional. The luminal N-terminal domain is extracellular-facing.
